author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
title: The Ninth Passage.

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: “This passage, too,” says he, “is quoted by them: ‘It is not of him that willeth, nor of him that runneth, but of God that showeth mercy.’”

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: And he observes that the answer to be given to them is derived from the same apostle’s words in another passage: “Let him do what he will.”

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: And he adds another passage from the Epistle to Philemon, where, speaking of Onesimus, [St. Paul says]: “‘Whom I would have retained with me, that in thy stead he might have ministered unto me in the bonds of the gospel.

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: But without thy mind would I do nothing; that thy benefit should not be as it were of necessity, but willingly.’

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: Likewise, in Deuteronomy: ‘Life and death hath He set before thee, and good and evil: . . .choose thou life, that thou mayest live.’

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: So in the book of Solomon: ‘God from the beginning made man, and left him in the hand of His counsel; and He added for him commandments and precepts: if thou wilt—to perform acceptable faithfulness for the time to come, they shall save thee.

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: He hath set fire and water before thee: stretch forth thine hand unto whether thou wilt.

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: Before man are good and evil, and life and death; poverty and honour are from the Lord God.’

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: So again in Isaiah we read: ‘If ye be willing, and hearken unto me, ye shall eat the good of the land; but if ye be not willing, and hearken not to me, the sword shall devour you: for the mouth of the Lord hath spoken this.’”

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: Now with all their efforts of disguise they here betray their purpose; for they plainly attempt to controvert the grace and mercy of God, which we desire to obtain whenever we offer the prayer, “Thy will be done in earth as it is in heaven;” or again this, “Lead us not into temptation, but deliver us from evil.”

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: For indeed why do we present such petitions in earnest supplication, if the result is of him that willeth, and him that runneth, but not of God that showeth mercy?

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: Not that the result is without our will, but that our will does not accomplish the result, unless it receive the divine assistance.

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: Now the wholesomeness of faith is this, that it makes us “seek, that we may find; ask, that we may receive; and knock, that it may be opened to us.”

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: Whereas the man who gainsays it, does really shut the door of God’s mercy against himself.

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: I am unwilling to say more touching so important a matter, because I do better in committing it to the groans of the faithful, than to words of my own.
